questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: Inflation is rising again. Courses of action: I. Efforts should be made to find out the cause of it. II. Fiscal deficit should be controlled. III. Special efforts should be made towards irrigation facilities.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: It is rumoured that there is a bomb in the Rashirapati Bhavan. Courses of action: I. The President should be escorted to a safer area. II. A proper search for bomb should be carried out. III. No attention should be paid to such baseless rumours.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: Expert cricket, our sportsmen have fared badly in international events. Courses of action: I. All sportsmen (except those associated with cricket) should be stopped from getting any incentive. II. Special promotion drive should be made and encouragement sports. III. International coaching and technology should be made available to our sportsmen.

choices.e: Either I or II and III follow

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: There have been incessant rains in the north-eastern parts of our country. Chances of heavy floods are not being ruled out. Courses of action: I. The Army should be given a notice to be on alert. II. Precautionary measures should be taken. III. North-eastern parts of our country should be disconnected from the rest of India.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: Space research is valuable not only because of its importance in defence and communication but also because it has value as a potential forex earner too.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: India has been facing the problem of child labour despite several legislations. Courses of action: I. India should treat child labour as a trivial issue. II. Child labour should be declared civil offence. III. Children indulging in such activities should be punished.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: After the success of films such as Hum Aapke Hain Kaun and Dilwale Dulhaniya Le Jayenge good music and clean entertainment are back again. Courses of action: I. Movies having good music and clean entertainment should be promoted. II. The two movies mentioned above should be awarded for their clean entertainment. III. The government should ban movies that are not like these two films.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: Although launched with great fanfare, private mutual funds have performed badly. Courses of action: I. Private mutual funds should be banned. II. Operations of mutual funds should be controlled by the government. III. Mutual funds should be given the government's protection.

questions.subject: verbal
questions.subtopic_name: Statement and Course of Action Evaluation
questions.body: Statement: Despite several efforts, AIDS appears to be spreading rapidly in India. Courses of action: I. The government should confine people suffering from AIDS in a separate cell. II. An awareness drive should be launched to increase awareness about AIDS. III. Sexual promiscuity should be discouraged.
